title: Resistive memory-based zero-shot liquid state machine for multimodal event data learning
methods: resistive memory in-memory computing (40 nm, 512x512 crossbar)
CLIP-inspired contrastive loss for multimodal alignment
t-SNE dimensionality reduction for embedding visualization
MAC-operation counting for training/inference complexity benchmarking

data_scope.n_mnist: 10 digit classes, 34x34 px frames cropped to 16x16, event-camera encoding
data_scope.n_tidigits: 10 spoken-digit classes, 64 frequency bands x 129 time steps
data_scope.neural_recordings: 26 alphabet classes (22 seen + 4 unseen: S,T,U,V), intracortical microelectrode array
data_scope.hardware: 40 nm 256 kB resistive memory macro, 512x512 crossbar, 456x201 subarray used for LSM
data_scope.exact_sample_counts: not stated in main text; see Supplementary Tables 4-6

core_conclusion: A hardware-software co-design on a physical 40 nm 256 kB resistive memory chip implements a liquid state machine SNN encoder paired with trainable ANN projection layers, achieving zero-shot multimodal learning across visual, audio, and neural-recording event streams with 152.83-to-393.07-fold training cost reduction and 23.34-to-160-fold energy efficiency improvement over cutting-edge digital hardware, while matching software model accuracy.
why_it_matters: Repurposes memristor fabrication stochasticity as fixed reservoir weights eliminating programming cost; achieves cross-modal zero-shot transfer (visual, audio, neural) on real chip not simulation; reduces training MACs by three orders of magnitude vs. SRNN baselines; enables energy budgets compatible with implantable brain-machine interfaces.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary co-design spanning device physics, systems integration and ML methodology; theoretical reservoir-computing insight empirically instantiated on a real chip; rigorous multi-baseline benchmarks on training cost, energy and accuracy; addresses AI sustainability and medical translational value.
added_value_to_my_research: LSM event-stream encoding of sparse spatiotemporal urban data (mobile-phone bursts, GPS trajectories) for real-time GeoAI inference on low-power edge nodes
In-memory Bayesian active learning extension for labeling-efficient fine-tuning of geospatial foundation models on satellite imagery
Graph-structured LSM input for spatiotemporal sensor networks (disease transmission, mobility flows between census tracts)
Random-sketch frontend as universal spatiotemporal feature compressor feeding explainable ML (MGWR, gradient-boosted trees) for spatial prediction
Efficiency-vs.-accuracy Pareto frontier methodology applicable to EV-charging efficiency-equity optimization and urban AI edge deployment

related.title: Energy-efficient multimodal zero-shot learning using in-memory reservoir computing
related.relation: companion paper addressing same multimodal zero-shot problem with a different in-memory reservoir architecture on overlapping datasets

related.title: Fully analogue reinforcement learning with memristors
related.relation: analogue memristor-based learning that could replace the fixed reservoir with an online-adapting one

related.title: Deep Bayesian active learning using in-memory computing hardware
related.relation: in-memory Bayesian active learning to reduce labeling cost for the contrastive projection head

related.title: Collective dynamics and long-range order in thermal neuristor networks
related.relation: graph-structured neuristor networks for collective sensor intelligence, extending the flat LSM input topology

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: foundational in-memory deep learning approach with biologically inspired dynamics that contextualises this paper's SNN-in-memory design

related.title: Random sketch learning for deep neural networks in edge computing
related.relation: random-sketch projection complementary to LSM random reservoir for ultra-low training cost on high-dimensional data

figures.caption: Hardware-software co-design: chip photo, TEM cross-sections of the memristor cell, conductance map of the 456x201 subarray, and cycle-to-cycle stability over 30,000 read cycles

N-MNIST supervised classification: spike embeddings (t-SNE), confusion matrix, accuracy vs. baselines, training-complexity breakdown (~802-818x reduction over SRNN), and inference energy (0.14 uJ hybrid vs. 4.4 uJ digital)
N-TIDIGITS supervised audio classification: event waveform, 3D t-SNE embeddings, confusion matrix, 22-fold energy savings over digital
Zero-shot visual-audio transfer: t-SNE alignment of seen/unseen class embeddings, 88% zero-shot accuracy, 152.83-fold training cost reduction, 23.34-fold energy gain

Brain-machine interface zero-shot transfer: neural recording + E-MNIST alignment, top-1/top-5 accuracy for 22+4 alphabet classes, 393.07-fold training reduction, >160-fold energy efficiency vs. digital
